title: ERRO[0030] unable to write container terminal output: resource temporarily unavailable
body:
**Description** While installing packages via `buildah run`, the program appears to lock up. This error message is displayed in the terminal. ``` ERRO[0030] unable to write container terminal output: resource temporarily unavailable ``` It just stays on that error permanently until I ctrl+c the process. With the error on the screen the working container is up. ``` # buildah containers CONTAINER ID BUILDER IMAGE ID IMAGE NAME CONTAINER NAME 1c9542922661 * 801894bc0e43 registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:27 fedora-working-container ``` **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. `buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:27` 2. `buildah run fedora-working-container -- dnf --assumeyes install bash-completion dnf-utils less procps-ng python2-rpm-macros python3-rpm-macros rpmdevtools rpmlint tree vim-enhanced wget` It does *not* happen every time, but rather about half of the times I try to reproduce it on my system. **Describe the results you received:** `ERRO[0030] unable to write container terminal output: resource temporarily unavailable` **Describe the results you expected:** command to finish successfully **Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:** ``` buildah-1.1-1.gitfbf46d3.fc27.x86_64 ```
Output of buildah version: ``` Version: 1.1 Go Version: go1.9.6 Image Spec: 1.0.0 Runtime Spec: 1.0.0 CNI Spec: 0.3.1 libcni Version: v0.6.0 Git Commit: fbf46d3 Built: Sun Jun 10 16:30:19 2018 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ```
Output of cat /etc/*release: ``` Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven) NAME=Fedora VERSION="27 (Twenty Seven)" ID=fedora VERSION_ID=27 PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 27 (Twenty Seven)" ANSI_COLOR="0;34" CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:27" HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/" SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help" BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/" REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora" REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=27 REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora" REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=27 PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy" Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven) Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven) ```
Output of uname -a: ``` Linux tassadar 4.16.14-200.fc27.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Jun 5 16:30:48 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux ```
Output of cat /etc/containers/storage.conf: ``` # storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools # that share the containers/storage libraries # See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information # The "container storage" table contains all of the server options. [storage] # Default Storage Driver driver = "overlay" # Temporary storage location runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage" # Primary Read/Write location of container storage graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage" [storage.options] # AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores # Must be comma separated list. additionalimagestores = [ ] # Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image. Only supported by # certain container storage drivers. size = "" # OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version override_kernel_check = "true" ```

comments.body:
If this is repeatable, could you add the Dockerfile you are using and run
buildah --debug bud ...

@carlwgeorge Thanks for the info report.  I didn't do exhaustive testing, will pick up in the morning.  But I wasn't able to reproduce this after 5 or 6 tries.  Will keep at it.  If you've the time, can you try the following?  It should accomplish the same thing for you and might make testing on your end a bit easier.

```
# ctr=$(buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:27)
# mnt=$(buildah mount $ctr)
# dnf --assumeyes --installroot=$mnt install bash-completion dnf-utils less procps-ng python2-rpm-macros python3-rpm-macros rpmdevtools rpmlint tree vim-enhanced wget

```

And in case it helps, your original test is a bit more repeatable with:

```
# ctr=$(buildah from registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:27)
# buildah run $ctr -- dnf --assumeyes install bash-completion dnf-utils less procps-ng python2-rpm-macros python3-rpm-macros rpmdevtools rpmlint tree vim-enhanced wget
```

Yeah I've been trying to reproduce it again with the `--debug` flag and it's eluding me.

I've been using dnf in the container because I use this same method to create CentOS images with the image's yum.  I'll see if I can reproduce it both ways.

@carlwgeorge Probably silly question time.  Can you verify that you've disk space available on your system?  I ran into similar errors during my bigger test as my disk ran out of space.  Probably not the case, but good to eliminate it just in case.

Disk space is fine, around 45% full.

I noticed the strangest thing.  While I was trying to capture debug output, I had zoomed in my terminal emulator to make the text more readable.  But when zoomed in, I can't reproduce the error.  After resetting the terminal zoom back to default the error happens again.  I observe the same behavior in both gnome-terminal and tilix.

I was able to trim down the package install list to just rpmdevtools and still trigger the error.  But somehow I doubt the packages matter, as long as there are a significant number of them.

Anyways, here's the debug output as requested.

On a different machine that runs Fedora 28, I wasn't able to reproduce the error at the default terminal zoom.  But if I zoomed out (made the text smaller), the error appeared!  This definitely appears to be related to the size of the terminal characters, or perhaps the number of rows and columns.  The from image doesn't seem to matter, as I was able to reproduce with several different combinations.

- Fedora 27 host with Fedora 27 image
- Fedora 27 host with Fedora 28 image
- Fedora 27 host with CentOS 7 image
- Fedora 28 host with Fedora 27 image
- Fedora 28 host with Fedora 28 image

In all scenarios, rolling back to buildah-1.0-1.git1ab80bc makes the problem go away.

@carlwgeorge Could you remove the return here and see if the error repeats itself?

buildah/run.go
I am now attempting with the terminal text small and the screen large to see if I can get a repeater.
All I can say is Great Catch! @carlwgeorge .  Thanks very much for the reproducer and it looks like @rhatdan is closing in on a fix.
I know where the break is, but I failed to figure out a workaround or fix.  Sent it off to @nalind to see if he has any great ideas.
One strange thing,was when I added fmt.Println() to the code, it suddenly worked.
@nalind added a fix for this, it will be in buildah 1.2 release.  @carlwgeorge You could also try it from master if you want.
